hydrogeofetch 2.0.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
  • Changelog

NA

Rename to hydrogeofetch

nhdplusTools is being renamed to hydrogeofetch (hydrologic geospatial fabric extraction tool chain) with a 2-year sunset ending October 2028, at which point nhdplusTools will be archived on CRAN.

Branches

  • nhdplusTools-1.5 — final CRAN release state of nhdplusTools (v1.5.0). Frozen; critical bug-fix patches only.
  • main — development branch, GitHub-latest. General maintenance and integration.
  • hydrogeofetch — active development toward hydrogeofetch v1.0.

Rename plan

  • Pure rename: function names and signatures stay identical in hydrogeofetch v1.0. No renames or signature changes during the sunset — the shim depends on stability. Cleanup happens post-archive or in hydrogeofetch 2.0 with its own deprecation cycle.
  • hydrogeofetch v1.0 is a drop-in equivalent with some non-breaking updates.
  • nhdplusTools API is frozen. Critical bug fixes only — no features, no refactors, unless explicitly asked.
  • Shim: once hydrogeofetch is on CRAN, nhdplusTools becomes function-level .Deprecated() aliases forwarding to hydrogeofetch::*. Maintained as a branch in the renamed repo.
  • Repo: rename in place to hydrogeofetch (preserves issues, PRs, redirects). Timing within the sunset window.
  • Cache: hydrogeofetch replaces the cache layer with no migration; users re-download on switch.
  • Reverse deps (small, friendly): amadeus, elfgen, StreamCatTools — coordinate directly.
  • DOIs: USGS-minted. hydrogeofetch gets a new USGS software release on code.usgs.gov with its own DOI. The existing nhdplusTools DOI will redirect to a historical release page for hydrogeofetch.
  • USGS software release for hydrogeofetch starts as soon as the renamed code is on GitHub, before CRAN submission.

When working in this repo, treat nhdplusTools as frozen and hydrogeofetch v1.x as the future canonical line. Avoid signature changes to public functions — they break the shim.

Reverse dependencies

Three packages depend on nhdplusTools/hydrogeofetch directly. Small and friendly — coordinate directly rather than waiting on CRAN policy.

  • amadeus (NIEHS) — uses get_huc
  • elfgen (HARPgroup) — uses get_nhdplus
  • StreamCatTools (USEPA) — uses get_waterbodies, navigate_nldi, get_nldi_basin, get_nhdplus, discover_nhdplus_id

None call any function removed in the milestone 4 hydroloom-deduplication pass (get_UT/get_UM/get_DM/get_DD, the indexing functions, navigation/network-attribute wrappers) — confirmed by grepping each repo’s source. Before removing or changing the signature of a function any of them call, re-check their source rather than assuming this list is still current.

CI and release

GitHub (doi-usgs/nhdplusTools) is the development platform. code.usgs.gov (code.usgs.gov/water/nhdplusTools) is the release platform. The GitLab CI pipeline (.gitlab-ci.yml) only runs on rc/* branches and produces a verified source tarball in the GitLab package registry. GitHub Actions runs R CMD check on push/PR to main for development.

Local builds are not part of the release workflow. Use devtools::test(), devtools::check(), and devtools::document() for local development.

Commands

  • devtools::test()
  • devtools::check() — R CMD check
  • devtools::document() — rebuild roxygen docs

Web service calls

Every function that hits a web service must validate the response shape strictly and degrade gracefully — \donttest examples are allowed to call live services on CRAN, but the underlying functions must fail cleanly rather than crash when those services misbehave. Don’t assume the server returned the JSON/GeoJSON schema you expect: check content type and structure before parsing, and return a well-defined empty/NULL value (not a malformed object) on degraded or unexpected responses. Failing/degraded services produce odd error conditions that are very hard to test exhaustively — strict response validation at the boundary is the only durable defense. Do not convert \donttest → \dontrun to dodge this; harden the caller.

Style

  • 2-space indent, snake_case for functions and variables
  • <- for assignment (not =)
  • |> native pipe in new code (%>% still in legacy code — don’t convert)
  • if( with no space before paren; K&R braces (} else { on same line)
  • One-liner if without braces OK: if(is.null(x)) stop("msg")
  • Validation: if (...) stop() — no assertion library. Concatenate with multiple args to stop(), not paste0().
  • message() for progress/status; warning(..., call. = FALSE) for empty results
  • Roxygen: @title sentence-case, @param type. Description, @inherit/@inheritParams to share docs
  • Visibility: @export for public API, @noRd for private helpers (most common), @keywords internal for internal-but-documented workers
  • Imports: @importFrom pkg fn in roxygen; pkg::fn() in body for explicitness (especially sf::, dplyr::, httr::)
  • .data$col pronoun inside dplyr verbs; !! for injection
  • Domain acronyms as-is: AOI, COMID, HUC, VAA, NLDI, VPU, NHD
  • Function args: continuation lines use 2-space indent (Positron/tidyverse style, not aligned to () in new code
  • Minimal diffs: don’t restyle existing code; only apply new style to new/changed lines
  • No trailing commas in lists or function signatures
  • Tests: skip_on_cran() for live API tests, testthat + expect_*

Contents

Developed by David Blodgett.
